item.title: When Should Your Kid Get a Smartphone? Scientists Have an Answer
item.description: Thirteen. At the earliest.

item.title: Brain Implants Are Unlocking a New Human-Machine Reality
item.description: The brain has emerged as a new frontier in medical technology. Bloomberg Primer explores where the next neurotechnology advances will lead.

item.title: Prospects for Job Seekers Seen Worsening, NY Fed Survey Says
item.description: Americans’ view of the labor market grew somewhat more pessimistic in May, with the perceived prospects for job seekers hitting their lowest point this year, according to a Federal Reserve Bank of New York survey released Monday.

item.title: Cipher Sells Junk Bonds to Fund Amazon-Tied Data Center Project
item.description: Cipher Digital Inc. is planning to raise $810 million from a junk-bond sale to help fund a data center tied to Amazon.com Inc.

item.title: Citadel Securities Sees Risk of Fed Forced to Raise Rates Soon
item.description: The next big risk confronting investors is tightening financial conditions as the Federal Reserve may need to raise interest rates “soon” to curb mounting inflation pressures, according to Citadel Securities.

item.title: AIP Leads Lenders Set to Take Control of Chemicals Firm ICP
item.description: American Industrial Partners is leading a group of creditors negotiating a deal with Innovative Chemical Products Group to swap their existing debt for equity of the coatings and adhesives maker, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Tango Soars as Study Shows Its Drug Can Help Shrink Tumors
item.description: Tango Therapeutics Inc. jumped as much as 48% after initial results of a study showed a combination of its drug and a Revolution Medicines Inc. medicine shrunk tumors in advanced pancreatic cancer patients.

item.title: G7 Ministers Seek Joint Fertilizer Action as War Roils Supplies
item.description: Group of Seven nations are weighing joint action to protect farmers and bolster food security as the war in the Middle East disrupts fertilizer supplies.

item.title: Mehta Joins Citigroup’s Treasury as Head of Portfolio Management
item.description: Ankur Mehta is joining Citigroup Inc.’s treasury department as head of portfolio management after leading the bank’s securitized products and credit research, according to an internal memo seen by Bloomberg.

item.title: Ratcliffe’s Ineos Kicks Off Loan Deal After Iran War Boost
item.description: Ineos Group started marketing new term loans to raise around $960 million, marking the second time in recent weeks that Jim Ratcliffe’s struggling chemicals conglomerate has tapped debt markets.

item.title: Texas Gas Drillers Shut Out of Oil Price Rally Turn to Shutting Off Wells
item.description: Two extremes are currently playing out in the Permian Basin. Drillers are reaping the benefits of a historic price rally due to the Iran war. But when it comes to natural gas, they’re having to pay customers to take it away.

item.title: Europe’s Next IPOs Will Add to the Year’s Focus on Defense
item.description: A familiar trend is set to dominate Europe’s batch of initial public offerings over the coming weeks: companies benefiting from higher defense spending by the continent’s governments.

item.title: Apple’s WWDC to Unveil New AI, Siri and iOS 27 Features
item.description: The crowd during the 2025 Apple Worldwide Developers Conference in Cupertino, California. Photographer: Justin Sullivan/Getty Images

item.title: FTX Co-Founder Sam Bankman-Fried Applies for Trump Pardon
item.description: FTX co-founder Sam Bankman-Fried formally applied for a presidential pardon, more than two years after he was convicted over the multi-billion dollar collapse of his once-thriving cryptocurrency empire.

item.title: Top Brazil Broker Tells Stock Traders: Don’t Just Buy the Index
item.description: Investors should get pickier with Brazil stocks as economic growth is set to cool and the nation heads toward a polarized presidential vote, says XP Inc.

item.title: India’s Current Account Gap Beats Forecasts as Remittances Surge
item.description: India’s current account fared better than expected in the year ended March, with the deficit staying flat as strong services receipts and higher remittances offset disruptions caused by the Iran war.

item.title: Woodford Sued by UK for Alleged Unauthorized Investment Advice
item.description: Britain’s financial watchdog sued Neil Woodford, the once-renowned investment manager, and his firm over allegations that he’s providing regulated investment advice without authorization.

item.title: A Data Center Is a Bank
item.description: Maybe we need universal basic tokens.

item.title: US Stocks Rebound From Selloff as Nvidia Leads Big-Tech Gains
item.description: US stocks bounced back on Monday from the worst rout this year, as a selloff in technology stocks eased and traders assessed flaring tensions in the Middle East, which supported oil prices and energy shares.

item.title: Google Tapped Intel for Over 3 Million Chips, Information Says
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google recently placed an order with Intel Corp. to manufacture more than 3 million of its specialized AI chips in 2028, the Information reported, citing two unnamed sources.

item.title: Europe’s Imports of Diesel and Jet Fuel Shrivel as War Drags On
item.description: Europe’s imports of diesel and jet fuel stagnated for a second month in May, piling pressure on regional energy supplies as the Iran war grinds on.

item.title: Fed Rate Hike Odds Rise as Strong Jobs Data Lowers Bar for Action
item.description: The bar for a Federal Reserve rate hike is falling as the job market remains robust in the face of stubborn price pressures, according to Collin Martin at the Schwab Center for Financial Research.

item.title: Amazon Seeks to Raise at Least C$7 Billion in Canada Bond Sale
item.description: Amazon.com Inc. is seeking to raise at least C$7 billion ($5 billion) from investment-grade bonds in Canadian dollars as US hyperscalers look beyond their home market to fund artificial intelligence spending.

item.title: UK’s Crown Estate Plans to Resell Abandoned Wind Farm Site
item.description: The British monarch’s commercial property and land manager plans to bring back to market the rights to develop a major wind farm in the Irish Sea after the former developers of the site gave up on the project last year.

item.title: Latest Houthi Shipping Threat Is Blunted by Thin Red Sea Traffic
item.description: Transits into the Gulf of Aden remain far below levels seen back in 2023, when the militants lashed out at commercial shipping.

item.title: Peru Bonds Edge Lower as Election Result Hangs in the Balance
item.description: Peruvian bonds edged lower as the country’s bitterly contested presidential election remained too close to call with the latest data showing conservative Keiko Fujimori holding the narrowest of leads over leftist Roberto Sánchez.

item.title: Chile Inflation Undershoots All Forecasts Before Rate Decision
item.description: Chile’s consumer prices rose much less than forecast last month, as the central bank monitors the inflationary fallout from a fuel cost hike before next week’s interest rate decision.

item.title: Brazil Economists Lift Key Rate Forecasts as Economy Stays Hot
item.description: Brazil’s economists raised their benchmark interest rate forecasts for this year and next as Latin America’s biggest economy holds up against tight financial conditions ahead of presidential elections.

item.title: US Urges NATO Allies to Use Defense Funds to Replace Huawei Gear
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration urged NATO allies to channel defense spending toward efforts to replace components from China’s Huawei Technologies Co. in their networks and critical infrastructure, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for June 8, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures are up 0.6% at 7:49 a.m. in New York, signaling a modest rebound for stocks after Friday’s slide.
